data.title: Uber, WeRide launch autonomous Robotaxi passenger ride service in Saudi Arabia
data.description:
Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) and WeRide (NASDAQ:WRD) have started offering autonomous Robotaxi passenger rides in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia.

The launch marks the first time autonomous vehicles are publicly available on the Uber platform in the Gulf country.

Starting today, Uber riders traveling between Roshn Front and Princess Noura University may be able to select a WeRide Robotaxi GXR.

At the initial launch, each Robotaxi is expected to include a vehicle operator and then transition to fully driverless operations in the future.

WRD shares were +3.06% Friday pre-market to $10.43, while UBER was +0.81% to $95.38.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow futures jump as retail inflation comes in cooler
data.description:
Stock index futures rose on Friday as the September consumer price index came in a little cooler than expected, while market sentiments also got a boost from Intel's (INTC) results. 

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.8%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +1.1%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.5%.

Wall Street finished higher on Thursday, as investors assessed Tesla's ((TSLA)) earnings and received news that President Donald Trump will meet with President Xi next Thursday.

Intel (INTC) shares rose 5.8% in premarket trade after the semiconductor giant posted third-quarter results and guidance that topped Wall Street's expectations.

The delayed CPI report finally landed and came in slightly cooler. CPI rose +0.3% M/M vs. 0.4% consensus and +0.4% in August. CPI rose +3.0% Y/Y vs. +3.1% consensus and +2.9% prior.

Core CPI came in at +0.2% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.3% prior, and Y/Y, +3.0% vs. +3.1% consensus and +3.1% prior.

The report was delayed due to the ongoing U.S. government shutdown

In more political news, Trump said Thursday night that he was immediately ending all trade negotiations with Canada because the Ontario provincial government aired an ad featuring former President Ronald Reagan speaking negatively about tariffs.

"Markets may be more concerned about increased policy uncertainty, in particular ahead of a scheduled meeting between Trump and China’s President Xi. Increased uncertainty or antagonism in the Sino-U.S. trade relationship would potentially have more serious economic consequences," Donovan said.

data.title: Aclara Resources unveils plan for $277M rare earths facility in Louisiana
data.description:
Aclara Resources (OTCPK:ARAAF) said Friday it plans to build a $277 million heavy rare earths separation facility in Louisiana, the first of its type in the U.S.

Aclara (OTCPK:ARAAF) said the plant will process material from the company's clay deposits in Brazil and Chile for magnets used in electric vehicles and wind turbines, with construction expected to be completed by end-2027, although the project still needs to secure funding and offtake agreements.

The state of Louisiana is supporting the project with $46.4 million in tax incentives and grants.

If the plant achieves full production, Aclara (OTCPK:ARAAF) anticipates being able to supply more than 75% of the U.S. requirements for minerals dysprosium and terbium for electric vehicles by 2028.

Aclara (OTCPK:ARAAF), which is 57% owned by the Hochschild Group, has a $1 billion-plus plan to mine rare earths in Latin America and develop processing facilities in the U.S.

The company secured as much as $5 million in funding last month from the U.S. International Development Finance Corporation for a Brazilian mining project, in an arrangement that includes an option to acquire shares in the company.

data.title: AlphaTON Capital appoints Wes Levitt as chief financial officer
data.description:
• AlphaTON Capital (ATON) has appointed Wes Levitt as chief financial officer, effective immediately.
  • Levitt was most recently Co-CIO at Alpha Sigma Capital Advisors, where he led investment strategy, asset allocation, and portfolio oversight for one of the blockchain industry’s most respected funds. He served as Head of Strategy at Theta Labs, where he directed global corporate strategy, marketing, analytics, and token infrastructure initiatives, helping to scale one of the most recognized decentralized video and data delivery platforms.
  • ATON +4.49% premarket to $5.35.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Alaska Air's Q3 profits squeezed by higher costs, expected to impact Q4 and FY25
data.description:
Shares of Alaska Air Group (NYSE:ALK) are trading defensively early Friday as the carrier’s below-consensus profit outlook overshadowed mixed third quarter results.

Coupled with an  IT outage overnight that grounded flights for approximately 8 hours, shares are under pressure and expected to trade in the red for a third day. And while the outage was not a result of a cyberattack, it infiltrated Alaska Air’s (NYSE:ALK) primary data center, impacting both Alaska Air and Horizon Air. This was the second outage the carrier suffered since this summer.

Alaska Air (ALK) delivered mixed results for the quarter as a better-than-expected 22.8% increase in revenue in the third quarter only resulted in profits of $1.05 per share, less than half of what it earned in the same quarter last year and at the low end of the company’s projected range of $1.00 to $1.40. Wall Street expected earnings of $1.10.

Similar to United Airlines (UAL) revenue growth was fueled by increased corporate and premium travel. However, Alaska (ALK) faced increased costs ((CASM)), declining revenue per available seat mile ((RASM)), dramatically compressing the company’s margins by as much as 840 basis points.

The higher costs for fuel, labor, and maintenance led the carrier to set its adjusted profit guidance for Q4 and FY25 below expectations. Alaska (ALK) expects to earn a profit of at least $0.40 for the fourth quarter and at least $2.40 for the year versus estimates of $0.88 and $2.93, respectively.

Additionally, RASM is estimated to be up by just low single digits for both Q4 and FY25 while CASM ex-fuel is projected to increase by low single digits in the current quarter and up mid-single digits for the year.

Shares last traded with a loss of nearly 5% putting the stock on track to open at its lowest level since April.

data.title: Writers Guild of America says it will work to stop Paramount from buying Warner Bros.
data.description:
The Writers Guild of America, the labor union representing professional writers in the film, television, and radio industries, has said it will work with regulators to block Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ:PSKY) from buying rival media giant Warner Bros. Discovery (NASDAQ:WBD), according to several media reports from late Thursday.

"Merger after merger in the media industry has harmed workers, diminished competition and free speech, and wasted hundreds of billions of dollars better invested in organic growth," the labor union reportedly said in a statement.

"Combining Warner Bros. with Paramount or another major studio or streamer would be a disaster for writers, for consumers, and for competition," the labor union said. 

The statement comes after news that David Ellison's Paramount was among the top suitors for WBD.

Deep-pocketed Ellison started the bid at $20, then rebuffed his offer between $22 and $24 per share. Following that, reports suggested the Paramount CEO offered $25 but was reluctant to pay more than that. WBD swiftly rejected all three offers from the company. 

Due to the high takeover interest in its world-class IP, WBD's top boss, David Zaslav, said earlier this week he is exploring strategic options for their assets but reaffirmed that the two-way split of the company is still on track.

Zaslav noted that WBD received multiple unsolicited interests for both the entire company and just its studio and streaming operations. CNBC had reported that Comcast (CMCSA) and Netflix (NFLX) were also among the parties interested in buying WBD's assets.

"It's no surprise that the significant value of our portfolio is receiving increased recognition by others in the market," Zaslav had said earlier.

data.title: Sierra Bancorp declares $0.25 dividend, up to 1M share repurchase authorization
data.description:
• Sierra Bancorp (BSRR) declared $0.25/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 3.76%
  • Payable Nov. 14; for shareholders of record Nov. 3; ex-div Nov. 3.
  • Additionally, the company said its board has authorized a share repurchase of up to 1M shares of common stock.
  • The new stock buyback program commences after the current share repurchase program expires on October 31, 2025, and continues until October 31, 2026. 
  • No shares remain available for repurchase under the current share repurchase program.
  • See BSRR Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Watch hospital stocks as HCA, Community Health post Q3 beats
data.description:
Hospital stocks are expected to trade higher on Friday after HCA Healthcare (NYSE:HCA), the number one for-profit healthcare provider in the U.S., and its peer Community Health Systems (NYSE:CYH) reported better-than-expected financials with their latest quarterly results.

Community Health Systems (NYSE:CYH) reported its Q3 2025 financials on Thursday after the close, exceeding Street forecasts for both revenue and earnings while raising its full-year outlook to a level above consensus.

HCA Healthcare (NYSE:HCA) added ~4% on Friday in the premarket, as its Q3 2025 financials also stood above expectations, and its revised earnings guidance for this year beat the consensus.

Their peers, such as Tenet Healthcare (THC) and Universal Health Services (UHS), are currently trading higher in the premarket after the announcements. Other hospital stocks to watch include Surgery Partners (SGRY), SunLink Health Systems (SHY), Select Medical Holdings (SEM), Acadia Healthcare (ACHC), and LifeStance Health Group (LFST).

For Q3, HCA reported roughly $19.2B in revenue, exceeding the consensus by $600M with ~10% YoY growth as its same facility revenue per equivalent admission grew ~6% YoY.

Meanwhile, HCA’s same-facility admissions and same-facility emergency room visits rose ~2% YoY and ~1%, respectively, and its same-facility inpatient and outpatient surgeries jumped ~1% YoY each, while the company reported ~50.6K licensed beds at the end of the quarter.  

Its adjusted quarterly EBITDA rose ~19% YoY to $3.9B, while adj. EBITDA margins improved to ~20% from ~19% in the year-ago period. The company reported $6.96 of non-GAAP earnings per share with ~42% YoY growth, exceeding the consensus by $1.92.

Looking ahead, HCA raised its full-year outlook for revenue and diluted EPS to $75.0B - $76.5B and $27.00 - $28.00 from $25.50 - $27.00 and $74.0B - $76.0B in the prior guidance and compared to $75.1B and $26.35 projected by analysts, respectively.
